records.ety: [L. fascinare; cf. Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/ to slander, bewitch.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fascinated (?), p. pr. & vb. n.. Fascinating (<?/).]

defs: To influence in an uncontrollable manner; to operate on by some powerful or irresistible charm; to bewitch; to enchant.
quotes: It has been almost universally believed that . . . serpents can stupefy and fascinate the prey which they are desirous to obtain.

defs: To excite and allure irresistibly or powerfully; to charm; to captivate, as by physical or mental charms.
quotes: There be none of the passions that have been noted to fascinate or bewitch but love and envy.
